title: Member Joining Form with Admin Dashboard Integration and Unique Member ID

requirements.text: Update the backend member registration logic to generate a unique member ID in the format 'RI' followed by a zero-padded 9-digit sequential number (e.g., RI 001920001). Store this formatted ID as part of the member record and return it upon successful registration.

quotes: Create unique id such as RI 001920001

acceptanceCriteria: Each newly registered member receives a unique ID in the format 'RI XXXXXXXXX' where X is a zero-padded 9-digit number.
IDs are sequential and do not repeat.
The unique ID is stored in the member record and retrievable via the backend.

requirements.text: Create a dedicated Member Joining Form page (or update the existing RegisterPage) that collects name, mobile number, email, and optional sponsor ID. On successful submission, display the newly generated unique member ID (e.g., RI 001920001) to the user. The form must validate all required fields before submission.

quotes: Create unique id such as RI 001920001

acceptanceCriteria: The joining form displays input fields for name, mobile, email, and optional sponsor ID.
Validation errors are shown for missing or invalid required fields.
On successful registration, the new unique member ID is displayed prominently to the user.
The form is accessible via the app navigation.

requirements.text: Ensure that upon successful member registration, the new member is immediately visible in the Admin Dashboard (AdminPanel) member list without requiring a manual refresh. The admin table must display the newly generated unique member ID (RI format) as a column.

quotes: After joining automatically add to admin dashboard

acceptanceCriteria: A newly registered member appears in the admin panel member list upon their next load or after triggering a refresh.
The admin member table includes a column showing each member's unique RI-format ID.
The admin panel React Query cache is invalidated after a new member is registered so the list reflects the latest data.

constraints: All backend logic must remain in the single Motoko actor at backend/main.mo.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
The unique member ID format must follow 'RI' prefix with a zero-padded 9-digit number.
nonGoals: Changing the existing commission or matrix tree logic.
Adding payment processing to the joining form.
Modifying the recruitment deadline or auto-cancellation features.
